products.name: QuickNav
products.descriptor: Low-SWaP satellite C2 access device for UAV BLOS command and control via Iridium

evidence.snippet:
QuickNav
========
A low-weight, all-in-one satellite command and control (C2) access device.

*   Beyond line of sight (BLOS) command and control (C2)
*   Standalone use for position beaconing (internal configuration and power)
*   Low size, weight, and power (SWaP)

products.name: ALTM GEN4
products.descriptor: Small form factor Iridium PNT system-on-module for GPS-denied and contested environments

evidence.snippet:
ALTM GEN4
---------
ALTM GEN4
The ALTM GEN4 family of products are standardized, small form factor Iridium PNT receivers that provide position, navigation, and timing (PNT) information in GPS-denied or contested environments.

products.descriptor: Iridium SBD satellite tracker family for asset and crew tracking

evidence.snippet:
9602
====
Lightweight satellite trackers delivering connectivity to high-value, untethered, and non-powered assets.

9602-LP, 9602-A, 9602-AB, 9602-BTI, 9602-NAL
The 9602 satellite trackers by NAL Technologies are low size, weight, and power (SWaP) solutions leveraging Iridium’s Short Burst Data®  (SBD®) services to provide cost-effective connectivity for commercial and government customers.

unresolved_questions: NDAA and Blue UAS compliance status is unknown for all 15 products — no compliance page was fetched and no product-level NDAA/Blue UAS claims appear in the corpus.
Product catalog pages 2–5 (naltec.com/products/page/2 through /page/5) were not fetched; additional products beyond the 15 prioritized may exist.

Individual product detail pages for 13 of the 15 products were skipped (only ALTM GEN2 Mini and 9602 had dedicated fetched pages), so specs, compliance claims, and readiness language for the remaining 13 come solely from short catalog-listing blurbs.
The Quicksilver™ product detail page was skipped (skipped_class_budget); its classification is based only on a brief related-product blurb on the 9602 page.

Vendor rebranded from Blue Sky Network to NAL Technologies (Naltec) in January 2026; some older product names (e.g. SkyLink Citadel) may map to current SKUs but could not be confirmed from the fetched corpus.
